zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William De Atherton IV於大約1405年出生在英國奧可福爾斯坦頓 哈爾庫特時，他的父親Sir William Atherton III24歲，母親Elizabeth Pilkington18歲。他於大約1440年與Lady Margaret Berkeley, born Byron在英國英格蘭艾可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1485年6月26日在法國享年81歲。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Atherton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from a place near Manchester named Atherton, from the Old English personal name Æthelhere + Old English tūn ‘settlement’.
English: habitational name from Hatherton, Staffordshire, from Old English haguthorn + dūn ‘the hill where hawthorn grows’.

English (East Anglia): topographic name from Middle English at ther doune ‘(dweller) on the hill’ (Old English æt thǣre dūne). Through confusion of -don and -ton, Attherdoune was altered to Atherton, Adderton, and Atterton.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William De Atherton IV was born about 1405, in Stanton Harcourt, Oxfordshire, England, his father, Sir William Atherton III, was 24 and his mother, Elizabeth Pilkington, was 18. He married Lady Margaret Berkeley, born Byron about 1440, in Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 26 June 1485, in France, at the age of 81.

Il a épousé Lady Margaret Berkeley, born Byron environ 1440, à Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 26 juin 1485, à France, à l’âge de 81 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William De Atherton IV(이)가 대략 1405년 영국 스퍼드실르 스탠턴 하르쿠르트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Atherton III(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Pilkington(은)는 18세였다. 그는 대략 1440년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 Lady Margaret Berkeley, born Byron(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1485년 6월 26일 프랑스에서 81세의 나이로 사망했다.
